rules.summary: Unless otherwise stated, this Part applies to:
In this part, the following definitions shall apply:
1. (1) Unless otherwise stated, in this Part, 2.1 to 2.5B, [2.13](/pra-rules/reporting
A firm must submit to the PRA information which is necessary for the purposes of the PRA’s supervision of the firm.
The information referred to in 2.1 must:
The information referred to in 2.1 and 2.2 must comprise the following:
The information which a firm submits to the PRA in accordance with 2.1 and [2.2](/pra-rules/reporting#3f50ae7540594ee08b62748e3f23194
A firm must have in place appropriate systems and structures to fulfil the requirements set out in 2.1 to [2.4](/pra-rules/reporting#e1
As part of the information referred to in 2.1, a firm is required to submit to the PRA on a regular basis:
1. (1) A firm must submit the ORSA report referred to in 2.5A(2)(a) within 10 business days after concluding the ORSA.

rules.summary: A firm must disclose publicly, on an annual basis, a SFCR.
The information which a firm discloses in its SFCR must:
A firm’s SFCR must contain the following information, either in full or by way of reference to equivalent information, both in nature and scope, disclosed publicly under other legal or regulatory
For the purposes of 3.3(1) the SFCR must include the following information regarding the business and performance of the firm:
For the purposes of 3.3(2), the SFCR must include the following:
For the purposes of 3.3(2), in respect of a firm’s risk profile, and 3.3(3), the SFCR
For the purposes of 3.3(4), where a firm applies:
For the purposes of 3.3(4), the SFCR must include the following:
In addition to the requirements of 3.5A below, the disclosure required by [3.3(5)(a)](/pra-rules/reporting#f9f02e827c024f71832f4f7785bc063b "3.
For the purposes of 3.3(5)(a), the SFCR must include the following information regarding the own funds of the firm:
For the purposes of 3.5A(7), the names of the counterparties shall not be disclosed where such disclosure is legally not possible or impractica
In addition to the requirements of 3.6A and 3.6B below, the disclosure required by [3.
For the purposes of the disclosure required by 3.3(5)(b), the disclosure of the amount of the SCR calculated using the firm’s internal model un
For the purposes of 3.3(5)(b), the SFCR must include the following information regarding the SCR and the MCR of the firm:
The disclosure of the SCR required by 3.3(5)(b) must be accompanied, where applicable, with a statement indicating that the final amoun
In addition to the disclosure required by 3.3(5)(c), where an internal model is used to calculate the SCR, the SFCR shall also include the foll
In addition to the disclosure required by 3.3(5)(d), the SFCR shall include the following information regarding any non-compliance with the MCR
The SFCR shall include any other material information, in a separate section, regarding the capital management of the firm.
Where a firm , in its SFCR , makes use of, or refers to, public disclosures made by the firm under other legal or regulatory requirements, those disclosures must be equivalent to the information
As soon as the SFCR, as well as any updated version of that report, is disclosed by a firm it shall be submitted to the PRA.

Without limiting the general application of 5.1, for the purposes of that rule the following will be regarded as a major development:
